questionText: What event happened on the day of Pentecost as described in Acts 2?
options: Jesus appeared visibly to all in Jerusalem
The holy spirit was poured out and the disciples spoke in different languages
The apostles performed a mass healing at the temple
An angel announced the resurrection to the crowd
explanation: On Pentecost 33 CE the holy spirit was poured out, the disciples spoke in tongues (different languages), and about 3,000 were baptised that day (Acts 2:1-4, 41).

options: Jesus erschien allen sichtbar in Jerusalem.
Der Heilige Geist wurde ausgegossen und die JÃ¼nger sprachen in verschiedenen Sprachen.
Die Apostel fÃ¼hrten im Tempel eine Massenheilung durch.
Ein Engel verkÃ¼ndete der Menge die Auferstehung.
de.explanation: Am Pfingsttag 33 n. Chr. wurde der Heilige Geist ausgegossen, die JÃ¼nger sprachen in Zungen (verschiedenen Sprachen), und etwa 3000 Menschen wurden an diesem Tag getauft (Apg 2,1-4.41).
de.questionText: Welches Ereignis trug sich am Pfingsttag zu, wie es in der Apostelgeschichte 2 beschrieben wird?

options: Jésus est apparu visiblement à tous les habitants de Jérusalem
Le Saint-Esprit a été répandu et les disciples ont parlé en différentes langues.
Les apôtres procèdent à une guérison collective au temple
Un ange annonce la résurrection à la foule
fr.explanation: Le jour de la Pentecôte 33 CE, le Saint-Esprit a été répandu, les disciples ont parlé en langues (différentes langues) et environ 3 000 personnes ont été baptisées ce jour-là (Actes 2:1-4, 41).
fr.questionText: Quel événement s'est produit le jour de la Pentecôte, tel qu'il est décrit dans Actes 2 ?

questionText: Peter quoted Psalm 16 in Acts 2 to prove what point about Jesus?
options: That Jesus was greater than Moses
That Jesus was the promised Messiah who would be resurrected
That Jesus had existed before Abraham
That Jesus fulfilled the Sabbath requirement
explanation: Peter argued that David, speaking prophetically, foretold the resurrection of the Christ â€” since David himself died and his tomb was still there, the prophecy must refer to Jesus (Acts 2:25-32).

questionText: What did God tell Moses the divine name is?
options: I Will Become What I Choose to Become (YHWH)
explanation: At the burning bush God revealed his name as 'I Will Become What I Choose to Become' (YHWH / Jehovah), emphasising his ability to fulfil his purposes.
sourceReference: Exodus 3:14-15; Insight on the Scriptures, Vol. 2, "Jehovah"

questionText: What is the first of the Ten Commandments?
options: You must have no other gods besides God
Do not take the name of God in vain
explanation: The first commandment is: 'You must have no other gods besides me' (Exodus 20:3), establishing exclusive devotion to Jehovah.

questionText: According to the Ten Commandments, what should be done on the Sabbath day?
options: Offer a sacrifice at the altar
Cease from all work and keep it holy
explanation: The fourth commandment required that no work be done on the Sabbath â€” neither by the Israelite, his household, nor his animals â€” because God rested on the seventh day (Exodus 20:8-11).

questionText: On which creative day did God make the sun, moon, and stars?
explanation: On the fourth day God made the two great luminaries and the stars to give light on the earth and to mark seasons, days, and years.

de.explanation: Am vierten Tag schuf Gott die beiden großen Leuchter und die Sterne, um die Erde zu beleuchten und die Jahreszeiten, Tage und Jahre zu kennzeichnen.
de.questionText: An welchem Tag schuf Gott die Sonne, den Mond und die Sterne?

fr.explanation: Le quatrième jour, Dieu a créé les deux grands luminaires et les étoiles pour éclairer la terre et marquer les saisons, les jours et les années.
fr.questionText: En quel jour Dieu a-t-il créé le soleil, la lune et les étoiles ?

questionText: What phrase is repeated after each day of creation to show God's approval?
explanation: After each creative work, the account says 'God saw that it was good,' showing divine satisfaction with his handiwork.

de.explanation: Nach jedem schöpferischen Werk heißt es im Bericht: "Gott sah, dass es gut war", was die göttliche Zufriedenheit mit seinem Werk zeigt.
de.questionText: Welcher Satz wird nach jedem Tag der Schöpfung wiederholt, um Gottes Zustimmung zu zeigen?

fr.explanation: Après chaque création, le récit dit que "Dieu vit que cela était bon", montrant ainsi la satisfaction divine face à son œuvre.
fr.questionText: Quelle phrase est répétée après chaque jour de la création pour montrer l'approbation de Dieu ?

questionText: In whose image was mankind created?
explanation: Genesis 1:27 states: 'God created man in his image, in God's image he created him; male and female he created them.'
sourceReference: Genesis 1:27; Insight on the Scriptures, Vol. 2, "Man"

de.explanation: In 1. Mose 1,27 heißt es: Gott schuf den Menschen als sein Abbild, als Abbild Gottes schuf er ihn; als Mann und Frau schuf er sie.
de.questionText: Nach wessen Bild wurde der Mensch geschaffen?
de.sourceReference: Mose 1:27; Einsicht in die Heilige Schrift, Band 2, "Der Mensch"

fr.explanation: La Genèse 1:27 stipule : "Dieu créa l'homme à son image, à l'image de Dieu, il le créa : Dieu créa l'homme à son image, il le créa à l'image de Dieu, il les créa homme et femme.
fr.questionText: À l'image de qui l'homme a-t-il été créé ?
fr.sourceReference: Genèse 1:27 ; Regard sur les Ecritures, vol. 2, "L'homme".

questionText: On which day were humans created?
explanation: According to Genesis 1:26-31, God created humans on the sixth day of creation.

de.explanation: Nach Genesis 1:26-31 schuf Gott den Menschen am sechsten Tag der Schöpfung.
de.questionText: An welchem Tag wurde der Mensch erschaffen?

fr.explanation: Selon la Genèse 1:26-31, Dieu a créé l'homme le sixième jour de la création.
fr.questionText: Quel est le jour de la création de l'homme ?

questionText: From what material did God form the first man?
explanation: Genesis 2:7 says God 'formed man from the dust of the ground' and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life.

questionText: What was the name of the garden where God placed Adam?
explanation: God planted a garden in Eden, eastward, and placed the man he had formed there (Genesis 2:8).

questionText: What did God say would happen to Adam if he ate from the tree of the knowledge of good and bad?
explanation: God warned: 'For in the day you eat from it you will certainly die' (Genesis 2:17). This introduced the prospect of death into human history.

questionText: What did God promise in Genesis 3:15 regarding the serpent's "offspring" and the woman's "offspring"?
options: The woman's offspring would crush the serpent's head
The serpent's offspring would rule the earth
Both would be removed from the garden
explanation: Genesis 3:15 records the first Messianic prophecy: the offspring of the woman would strike the serpent's head, pointing to the crushing of Satan by Jesus Christ.
sourceReference: Genesis 3:15; Revelation 12:9; Insight on the Scriptures, Vol. 1, "Enmity"

questionText: What did Jesus tell Nicodemus a person must do to see the Kingdom of God?
options: Be born again (from water and spirit)
Give all his possessions to the poor
explanation: Jesus said: 'Unless anyone is born of water and spirit, he cannot enter into the Kingdom of God' (John 3:5), referring to the anointing by holy spirit required for those who will rule in heaven.

questionText: What does John 3:16 say God gave because he loved the world so much?
explanation: 'For God loved the world so much that he gave his only-begotten Son, so that everyone exercising faith in him might not be destroyed but have everlasting life' (John 3:16).

questionText: What miracle did Jesus perform that demonstrated his power over death, shortly before his own death?
options: He raised Lazarus from the dead after four days
He restored sight to a man born blind
He fed 5,000 men with five loaves and two fish
explanation: Jesus raised Lazarus who had been in the tomb for four days (John 11:39, 43-44). This miracle strengthened faith and foreshadowed the resurrection Jesus himself would experience.

questionText: In his prayer to his Father in John 17, what did Jesus say constitutes everlasting life?
options: Keeping the Sabbath and the festivals
Taking in knowledge of the Father and the Son
explanation: Jesus prayed: 'This means everlasting life, their taking in knowledge of you, the only true God, and of the one whom you sent, Jesus Christ' (John 17:3). Knowing God and Christ is foundational to eternal life.

questionText: According to Jesus' Sermon on the Mount, who are "the meek"?
options: Those who hunger and thirst for righteousness
Those who will inherit the earth
explanation: Matthew 5:5: 'Happy are the mild-tempered, since they will inherit the earth.' The meek â€” those who are humble and submissive to God â€” receive the earth as their inheritance.

questionText: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus said his disciples are the "salt of the earth." What does this imply about their role?
options: They should be numerous like grains of salt
They preserve and add moral flavour to society
They should keep themselves pure and separate
They bring a sharp or difficult message
explanation: Salt was used for preservation and flavouring. Jesus meant his followers would exert a preserving, morally wholesome influence on those around them.

questionText: In the Lord's Prayer, what is the first petition Jesus taught his disciples to make?
options: Give us today our bread for this day
Deliver us from the wicked one
explanation: Matthew 6:9 shows the first petition is 'Let your name be sanctified' â€” honouring and clearing God's name comes before requests for personal needs.

questionText: What does Jesus say about storing up treasures on earth versus in heaven?
options: We should equally value both earthly and heavenly treasures
Heavenly treasures cannot be corrupted by moth, rust, or thieves
We should give half our wealth to the poor
explanation: Jesus contrasts earthly treasures â€” which rust, are eaten by moths, and can be stolen â€” with heavenly treasures, which are permanent. He concludes: 'Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also' (Matthew 6:20-21).

questionText: In Matthew 24, what sign did Jesus give that would indicate "the conclusion of the system of things"?
options: A combination of wars, food shortages, earthquakes, pestilences, and increased lawlessness
The rebuilding of the Jerusalem temple
A great comet appearing in the sky
explanation: Matthew 24:7-12 lists wars, food shortages, earthquakes, pestilences, false prophets, and increased lawlessness as parts of the composite sign of the last days.

questionText: How does David describe Jehovah at the opening of Psalm 23?
explanation: Psalm 23 opens: 'Jehovah is my Shepherd. I will lack nothing.' David draws on his own experience as a shepherd to illustrate God's tender care.

questionText: What does Psalm 23:4 say David will not fear, even when walking through the valley of deep shadow?
explanation: 'Even if I walk in the valley of deep shadow, I fear no calamity' â€” the phrase often rendered 'valley of the shadow of death' reflects trust in God's presence even in the darkest trials.

questionText: What request does Psalm 83 make regarding God's name?
options: That his name be praised in all the earth
That people may know that Jehovah alone is the Most High over all the earth
That his name be hidden from the nations
That his name be written in the heavens
explanation: Psalm 83:18 says: 'May people know that you, whose name is Jehovah, you alone are the Most High over all the earth.' This verse preserves God's personal name in many Bible translations.

questionText: What does Revelation 21:4 promise God will do away with?
options: The sun and moon (replaced by God's glory)
All memory of the former world
explanation: Revelation 21:4 promises: 'He will wipe out every tear from their eyes, and death will be no more, neither will mourning nor outcry nor pain be anymore.' This describes conditions in the new world.

questionText: What does the "new Jerusalem" descending from heaven in Revelation 21 represent?
options: A literal rebuilt city in Palestine
The heavenly government â€” Christ and his 144,000 co-rulers â€” coming to rule over the earth
The church as a spiritual community
The angels sent to protect the earth
explanation: The new Jerusalem symbolises the Kingdom of God â€” Jesus Christ and the 144,000 anointed ones â€” descending to bring righteous rule and blessing to all the earth (Revelation 21:2; 3:12).
sourceReference: Revelation 21:2; 3:12; Insight on the Scriptures, Vol. 2, "New Jerusalem"

questionText: What does the river of water of life in Revelation 22 flowing from God's throne represent?
options: Literal water for the new earth
God's life-giving provisions â€” including the ransom and Kingdom blessings â€” that restore humans to perfect life
The holy spirit flowing to the angels
The prayers of all the holy ones
explanation: The river of life proceeding from God's throne symbolises the life-giving blessings God provides through his Kingdom â€” ultimately leading to the full restoration of human life.
